goal: Package scene + assets as a self-contained deliverable the client can open without unresolved-reference hell.
thoughts: Last handoff broke on absolute paths. export_scene_package flattens refs to relative. Pre-audit with run_stage_analysis so Raj knows what is bundled vs what the client must install (Nucleus assets, custom extensions). Produce .usdz or .zip. Version-stamp: 5.1.0 scene opens in 5.0.0 only if no 5.1-specific schemas were used.
code:
# 1. See what external refs exist
run_stage_analysis()

# 2. Pack everything into a single deliverable
export_scene_package(
    stage_path="<STAGE_USD>",            # /home/raj/client_project/stage.usd
    output_path="<DELIVERABLE_ZIP>",     # /home/raj/deliveries/client_v3.zip
    format="<zip|usdz>",
    rewrite_refs_relative=True,
    include_textures=<True|False>,        # False to shrink; client must re-link
    isaac_sim_version="<5.1.0>"
)

# Verify: unzip to /tmp, grep for absolute paths before emailing
# run_usd_script to assert no path starts with '/home/raj'
failure_modes: Absolute paths leak through custom python modules, not USD refs
Nucleus omniverse:// URIs not resolvable on client's workstation
5.1-only schema (e.g. new PhysX feature) silently fails in 5.0.0
Stripped textures — client sees pink material and thinks it is broken
